title: Bridging open work permit for permanent residence applicants - Canada.ca
content:
## Bridging open work permit for permanent residence applicants
A bridging open work permit (BOWP) lets you keep working while you wait for the results of your permanent residence application.
You may be eligible for a BOWP if you applied to one of the permanent residence programs below.

## If you applied for Express Entry
These instructions are for permanent residence applications for the
- Federal Skilled Worker Program
- Canadian Experience Class
- Federal Skilled Trades Program

### Who can apply for a BOWP
To be eligible, you must
- live in Canada (and intend to live outside Quebec) at the time you apply for your BOWP You can leave Canada while we process your application. However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- You can leave Canada while we process your application.
- However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit
- have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker
- be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- be the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- have submitted a complete
                    application for permanent residence and passed the completeness check Submitting a profile to the pool is not the same as applying for permanent residence. Make sure you understand how
                            Express Entry works .
- Submitting a profile to the pool is not the same as applying for permanent residence.
- Make sure you understand how
                            Express Entry works .
- have your acknowledgement of receipt letter We send this letter to your account after we get your permanent residence application
- We send this letter to your account after we get your permanent residence application

### If you’re not eligible for a BOWP yet
Don’t let your work permit expire while you wait for an invitation to apply. Maintain your status in
                Canada by extending
                    your work permit .
Your employer may need to get a new Labour Market Impact Assessment or submit a new offer of employment
                for you to do this.

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of your acknowledgement of receipt letter
Upload your acknowledgement of receipt letter in the “Client information” field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you:
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for the Provincial Nominee Program via Express Entry
To be eligible for a BOWP, you must
- live in Canada (and intend to live outside Quebec) at the time you apply for your BOWP
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit, or have an expired work permit but maintained your status and the authority to work without a work permit, or be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit, or
- have an expired work permit but maintained your status and the authority to work without a work permit, or
- be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- be the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- have no employment restrictions as a condition of your nomination
- have submitted a complete
                    application for permanent residence and passed the completeness check Submitting a profile to the pool is not the same as applying for permanent residence. Make sure you understand how
                            Express Entry works .
- Submitting a profile to the pool is not the same as applying for permanent residence.
- Make sure you understand how
                            Express Entry works .
- have your acknowledgement of receipt letter We send this letter to your account after we get your application
- We send this letter to your account after we get your application
Don’t let your work permit expire while you wait for an invitation to apply. Maintain your status in
                Canada by extending
                    your work permit .
Your employer may need to get a new Labour Market Impact Assessment or submit a new offer of employment
                for you to do this.

#### Leaving Canada while your application is in process
You can leave Canada temporarily while we process your permanent residence application.
However, if your work permit expires while you’re outside Canada, or if you leave after it expires, you’ll lose your temporary resident status. If you return to Canada as a temporary resident, you won’t be able to work until your new work permit is approved.
You must be in Canada with valid TR status when we make a decision on your BOWP, or your application may be refused.

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of your nomination letter
- a copy of your acknowledgement of receipt letter
Upload your acknowledgement of receipt letter and your nomination letter in the “Client information”
                field.
Find out how to upload multiple
                files to 1 field in your online application .
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you:
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for the Provincial Nominee Program (not through Express Entry)
To be eligible for a BOWP, you must
- live in Canada (and intend to live outside Quebec) at the time you apply for your BOWP
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit, or have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker, or be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit, or
- have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker, or
- be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- be the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- have no employment restrictions as a condition of your nomination
- have submitted a complete application for permanent residence and passed the eligibility assessment
- have your acknowledgement of receipt letter We send this letter to your account after we get your application.
- We send this letter to your account after we get your application.

#### Leaving Canada while your application is in process
You can leave Canada temporarily while we process your permanent residence application.
However, if your work permit expires while you’re outside Canada, or if you leave after it expires, you’ll lose your temporary resident status. If you return to Canada as a temporary resident, you won’t be able to work until your new work permit is approved.
You must be in Canada with valid TR status when we make a decision on your BOWP, or your application may be refused.

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of your nomination letter
- a copy of the acknowledgement of receipt letter you received when you submitted your Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) permanent residence application This letter doesn’t mean your BOWP application is being processed. We won’t start processing your BOWP until we review your permanent residence application and confirm that you meet the basic eligibility requirements for PR under the PNP .
- This letter doesn’t mean your BOWP application is being processed. We won’t start processing your BOWP until we review your permanent residence application and confirm that you meet the basic eligibility requirements for PR under the PNP .
Upload your acknowledgement of receipt letter, which you received when you applied for permanent
                residence through the PNP, in the “Client information” field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for permanent residence as a Quebec skilled worker
To be eligible for a BOWP, you must
- live in Canada and intend to live in Quebec You can leave Canada while we process your application. However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- You can leave Canada while we process your application.
- However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- hold a Certificat de sélection du Québec (CSQ) Your CSQ must have been valid when you applied for permanent residence.
- Your CSQ must have been valid when you applied for permanent residence.
- be the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- have passed a completeness check for permanent residence
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit
- have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker
- be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of the letter that shows your permanent application number This number begins with the letter “E”
- This number begins with the letter “E”
Upload the letter that shows your permanent application number in the “Client information”
            field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you
- respond “yes” to the question Have you been told by an Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship
                    Canada office that you are approved in principle for permanent residence in Canada?
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for the Home Child Care Provider Pilot or Home Support Worker Pilot
To be eligible for a BOWP, you must
- live in Canada (and intend to live outside Quebec) at the time you apply for your BOWP You can leave Canada while we process your application. However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- You can leave Canada while we process your application.
- However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- be approved
                    in principle for permanent residence
- prove you have 6 months of qualifying work experience
- meet 1 of the following criteria: have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit have applied to extend your work permit before your original permit expired be eligible to restore your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit
- have applied to extend your work permit before your original permit expired
- be eligible to restore your status and get a work permit

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of the letter we send you after your permanent residence application is approved in principle
Upload your approval in principle letter in the “Client information” field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you:
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for the caring for children class or caring for people with high medical needs class
To be eligible for a BOWP, you must
- live in Canada (and intend to live outside Quebec) at the time you apply for your BOWP You can leave Canada while we process your application. However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- You can leave Canada while we process your application.
- However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit
- have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker
- be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- be the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- be approved
                    in principle for permanent residence
- have submitted a complete application for permanent residence before the programs closed on June 18,
                2019

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include:
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of the letter we send you after your permanent residence application is approved in principle
Upload your approval in principle letter in the “Client information” field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you:
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for the Agri-Food Pilot
To be eligible for a BOWP, you must
- live in Canada (and intend to live outside Quebec) at the time you apply for your BOWP You can leave Canada while we process your application. However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- You can leave Canada while we process your application.
- However, if you leave Canada after your work permit expires, you can’t work until your new
                        application is approved.
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit
- have an expired work permit but maintained
                            your status as a worker
- be eligible to restore
                            your status and get a work permit
- be the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- be approved
                    in principle for permanent residence

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of the letter we send you after your permanent residence application is approved in principle
                (approval in principle letter)
Upload your approval in principle letter in the “Client information” field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you
- select “Open work permit” as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply
                online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply
                on paper .

## If you applied for permanent residence as a Quebec investor
You may be eligible for a BOWP if you
- applied for permanent residence as a Quebec investor and passed a completeness check
- hold a valid Quebec Selection Certificate (Certificat de sélection du Québec [CSQ ]) or held a valid CSQ when you submitted your permanent residence application
- are the principal applicant on your permanent residence application
- either have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit have an expired work permit, but maintained your status as a worker, or are eligible to restore your status and get a work permit
- have valid temporary resident status and a valid work permit
- have an expired work permit, but maintained your status as a worker, or
- are eligible to restore your status and get a work permit
You also need to meet the general eligibility requirements for a work permit.

### Supporting documents
When you submit your BOWP application, include
- the supporting documents listed in the document checklist
- a copy of the letter that shows your permanent residence application number This number begins with the letter -E. When applying online, upload the letter in the “Client information” field.
- This number begins with the letter -E.
- When applying online, upload the letter in the “Client information” field.
Follow the instructions for extending or changing the conditions on your work permit.
When you apply, make sure you
- respond Yes to the question, “Have you been told by an Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada office that you are approved in principle for permanent residence in Canada?”
- select Open work permit as the type of work permit
- pay the work permit processing fee and the open work permit holder fee
In most cases, you must apply online .
If you’re having problems applying online, you can apply on paper .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
